root_text.sn52.4:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca anuruddho āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno sākete viharanti kaṇḍakīvane.
root_text.sn52.4:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahāmoggallāno sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhitā yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā anuruddhena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn52.4:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn52.4:1.4: “sekhenāvuso anuruddha, bhikkhunā katame dhammā upasampajja vihātabbā”ti?
root_text.sn52.4:2.1: “Sekhenāvuso sāriputta, bhikkhunā cattāro satipaṭṭhānā upasampajja vihātabbā.
root_text.sn52.4:2.3: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn52.4:2.6: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ—
root_text.sn52.4:2.7: sekhenāvuso sāriputta, bhikkhunā ime cattāro satipaṭṭhānā upasampajja vihātabbā”ti.

translation_text.sn52.4:1.1: At one time the venerables Anuruddha, Sāriputta, and Mahāmoggallāna were staying near Sāketa, in the Thorny Wood.
translation_text.sn52.4:1.2: Then in the late afternoon, Sāriputta and Mahāmoggallāna came out of retreat, went to Anuruddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn52.4:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side. Sāriputta said to Anuruddha:
translation_text.sn52.4:1.4: “Reverend Anuruddha, what things should a trainee mendicant enter and remain in?”
translation_text.sn52.4:2.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, a trainee mendicant should enter and remain in the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn52.4:2.3: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn52.4:2.4: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn52.4:2.6: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn52.4:2.7: A trainee mendicant should enter and remain in these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.”
